From Pelham Manor's historic estates to the charming homes near Colonial Avenue and the Hutchinson River Parkway corridor, we protect properties throughout this tight-knit Westchester community.

faqs.question: Why are silverfish common in Pelham homes?
faqs.answer: Pelham's many older homes with stone foundations, coupled with the area's humidity and mature landscaping, create perfect conditions for silverfish. These pests are attracted to damp basements, bathrooms, attics, and areas with poor ventilation commonly found in the community's historic properties.

faqs.question: How much does silverfish control cost in Pelham?
faqs.answer: Our silverfish control services in Pelham range from $350 to $550, depending on the size of your property, severity of the infestation, and treatment method required. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How quickly can you eliminate a silverfish infestation?
faqs.answer: Most silverfish infestations can be significantly reduced within 2-3 weeks of initial treatment. Complete elimination typically requires follow-up visits and addressing moisture issues. We provide a comprehensive treatment plan with timeline expectations during your initial inspection.

faqs.question: Do you offer preventative silverfish control in Pelham?
faqs.answer: Yes, we offer ongoing preventative maintenance plans that include regular inspections, moisture control recommendations, and perimeter treatments to keep silverfish out of your Pelham home year-round. Prevention is especially important in older Westchester properties prone to humidity issues.
